model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the button labeled 'Submit'
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (308,151) said dx=65 dy=115 conf=0.80 -> (373,266)
  iter 1: at (373,266) said dx=80 dy=-70 conf=0.80 -> (453,196)
  iter 2: at (413,231) said dx=-68 dy=-20 conf=0.90 -> (345,211)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants to click the 'Submit' button.
The current crosshair (red) is at (396, 226).
The 'Submit' button is the middle blue rectangle.
The crosshair is currently on the right side of the 'Submit' button.
The center of the 'Submit' button is to the left of the crosshair.
The 'Submit' button spans roughly from x=380 to x=530 (Wait, looking at the image).
Let's re-examine the button positions.
Cancel: left
Submit: center
Delete: right
Save: bottom
Crosshair is at (396, 226).
Looking at the image, the 'Submit' button center is further to the left than the crosshair.
Wait, the crosshair is at (396, 226).
The 'Submit' button's left edge is around 380, right edge is around 530? No, looking closer.
The 'Submit' button is centered. The crosshair is on the right side of the 'Submit' button.
Let's estimate the 'Submit' button center.
The button's x-range is approximately [380, 530]. No, that's not right.
Looking at the buttons:
Cancel: x from ~125 to ~275
Submit: x from ~380 to ~530
Delete: x from ~630 to ~780
Actually, the crosshair (396, 226) is near the left edge of the 'Submit' button.
Wait, looking at the image again.
The red crosshair is at (396, 226).
The 'Submit' button is the blue rectangle it is currently over.
The text 'Submit' is on the left side of that button.
The center of the 'Submit' button is to the right of the crosshair?
Actually, looking at the crop, the crosshair is inside the blue rectangle labeled 'Submit'.
The button seems to go from x=380 to x=530.
Center would be around (380+530)/2 = 455.
The crosshair is at 396.
So dx would be roughly 455 - 396 = 59.
Let's re-evaluate.
The blue circle is at the same position as the crosshair (396, 226).
The 'Submit' button center is to the right.
Looking at the text 'Submit', it starts at the left.
The button center is likely further right.
Let's estimate the button boundaries: left=380, right=530. Center x=455.
Current x=396. dx = +59.
y-axis: The button is centered vertically around 360-380? No, the coordinates are given as (396, 226).
The button center y is roughly 370? Wait.
The crosshair is at 226. The button looks centered vertically around that.
Let's check the y coordinate of the button.
The crosshair seems to be pretty much in the middle vertically.
Let's re-read the image carefully.
The crosshair is at (396, 226).
The 'Submit' button's center is clearly to the right.
Let's refine the button coordinates.
Left edge: ~380. Right edge: ~530.
Center x: ~455.
dx = 455 - 396 = 59.
dy = 0 (it looks vertically centered).
Confidence: 0.9.
